title: bridelia sikkimensis
intro: Bridelia sikkimensis, also known as Sikkim spurge, is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Bridelia sikkimensis has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an anti-cancer agent. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine practices.
